text.title: STT Integration Patterns and Production Rules
text.summary: Batch vs. streaming integration, large-file chunking with overlap, speaker diarization, SRT/VTT output formatting, and production rules for transcription reliability.

rule.statement: Validate file format and size before calling any STT API — return a structured error for unsupported inputs rather than letting the API fail mid-transfer.
rule.rationale: Large file uploads to APIs fail after consuming bandwidth. Pre-validation catches unsupported formats (.wav vs .wav64) and oversized files locally before any network cost is incurred.

list.item: OpenAI API limit: 25MB per file; chunk larger files to 5-minute segments with 5-second overlap
Overlap prevents mid-word cuts at segment boundaries; deduplicate at joins
Use verbose_json response format when word timestamps are needed (adds ~10% latency)
For language auto-detection, omit the language param; for forced language, always specify

rule.statement: Chunk large audio files to 5-minute segments with a 5-second overlap to prevent word truncation at boundaries.
rule.rationale: Without overlap, words that span a chunk boundary are split and the fragment gets dropped or mis-transcribed. A 5-second overlap ensures every word lands fully in at least one chunk.

example.note: Chunking with pydub and overlap join
example.code:
from pydub import AudioSegment import tempfile from pathlib import Path
def chunk_audio(path: str, chunk_ms: int = 300_000, overlap_ms: int = 5_000) -> list[str]: """Split audio into overlapping chunks; returns list of temp file paths.""" audio = AudioSegment.from_file(path) chunks = [] start = 0 while start < len(audio): end = min(start + chunk_ms, len(audio)) chunk = audio[start:end] with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".mp3", delete=False) as f: chunk.export(f.name, format="mp3") chunks.append(f.name) start = end - overlap_ms return chunks

list.item: AssemblyAI: pass speaker_labels=True in TranscriptionConfig; returns utterances with speaker A/B labels
Deepgram Nova-2: pass diarize=True in PrerecordedOptions; returns words with speaker field
OpenAI GPT-4o Transcribe: supports diarization natively; pass timestamp_granularities=["word","segment"]
Local: use pyannote-audio for diarization + faster-whisper for transcription; align by timestamp
Speaker identification (matching names to labels) requires a separate speaker enrollment step
SRT: sequential index + HH:MM:SS,mmm → HH:MM:SS,mmm + text + blank line; for video subtitles
VTT: WEBVTT header + HH:MM:SS.mmm → HH:MM:SS.mmm + text; for web <track> elements
JSON: full structured output with words, confidence, speaker labels; for downstream processing
Plain text: best for LLM input — no timestamps, just words; strip filler words in post-processing

rule.statement: Always configure a fallback provider; never let a single-provider outage block a transcription pipeline.
rule.rationale: OpenAI API has had multiple incidents where audio transcription endpoints were degraded while chat remained up. A fallback to faster-whisper or AssemblyAI eliminates single-point dependency with minimal code overhead.

list.item: Store transcription cost metadata (minutes, provider, model) alongside every transcript for audit and billing
For async workloads: accept file upload, return job_id, send results via webhook — never block HTTP for >30s
Rate limits: OpenAI 50 req/min; Deepgram 100 req/min; AssemblyAI 32 concurrent; build a semaphore gate
Clean up temp files in a finally block — chunking creates many temp files that accumulate on disk

text.reference: FastAPI endpoint with async + webhook delivery
